scope: Controlled Worker-version traffic rollback and restoration only. No D1, R2, KV, rate-limit, feature-flag, production, form, email, or notification mutation was performed. The rollback temporarily restored the earlier version-bound staging Slack secret configuration; no secret value was read, written, logged, or used.

preflight.result: Both versions expose the same D1, R2, KV, rate-limit, asset, Access, and feature-flag bindings. Both declare the same two staging secret names; secret values were neither read nor recorded.

limitations: This proves staging Worker traffic rollback and restoration only; it does not roll back or test restoration of D1, R2, KV, rate-limit, or secret values.
The older version temporarily carried its prior staging Slack configuration; the exact current version was restored before the rehearsal completed, and no notification was sent.
Production Worker traffic was not changed.
No customer journey, authenticated Access, email, Slack, or production migration gate is closed by this rehearsal.
